—
—
—
What this tab tests: Every Monday for the past year, we ran the forecast as if we'd run it that day, then compared its prediction to what actually shipped 30 days later. The chart below shows what was promised vs what was delivered at each confidence level. If the green bars match the gray bars, the forecast is honest.
When we say 50% sure
—
 
When we say 70% sure
—
 
When we say 85% sure
—
 
When we say 95% sure
—
 

Promised vs delivered (if the bars match, the forecast is honest)

Forecast vs reality, week by week (blue = what we'd have promised at 85% confidence · green = what actually shipped)

Calibration curve (diagonal line = perfect honesty · for the technically curious)

▸ Show the full forecast ledger (every Monday, every prediction, every outcome)
Forecast made 50% floor 70% floor 85% floor 95% floor Actual (next 30d) Hit?
Source: GovSpend Jira Cloud · Time-in-state from customfield_10023 ([CHART] Time in Status). WIP dwell from changelog. State mapping: Dev/PR/QA/UAT/Waiting (Failed QA + Deployment Plan excluded). Issue types: Story + Bug. Forecast uses bootstrap Monte Carlo (10,000 trials) sampled from daily throughput.
